dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, respond to a Person Down in the Park Lot near Willow Way. Dispatched at 16:38.
Hello, I'm Blake with EMS. What occurred here that made you call 911?
We found him unresponsive with strange breathing. He was okay 15 minutes ago.
Is he responsive - can he hear or respond to commands at all?
No, nothing. He doesn't move or react. Eyes closed the whole time.
Does he open his eyes? Even when we're touching him?
No eye movement. No response to touch or voice.
GCS is 3. Agonal respirations, no spontaneous movement.
Airway is patent, but unsupported. Need to assess for obstruction.
I'm initiating BVM ventilations. SpO2 has increased to 92%.
Breathing pattern is agonal at 12/min currently. No distinct asymmetry or adventitious sounds.
Strong bilateral radial pulses. Cap refill under 2 seconds. Warm, dry skin. Pinpoint pupils.
Heart rate is 90. Normal rhythm but elevated now.
Respiratory rate is 12/min with agonal breathing.
Blood pressure is 214/108 - significantly elevated.
Room air SpO2 is 100% right now.
Glucose is zero - need to recheck shortly.
EKG is sinus rhythm. 4-lead ECG started as per protocol.
4-lead EKG monitoring initiated.
Inserted 30Fr nasopharyngeal airway in the right nare.
ETCO2 reading is 40. Airway maintained and effective now.
Administering 1mg Narcan intranasally in the right nare.
Respirations have increased to 18/min. Better airflow.
Blood pressure is now 199/127. Still elevated.
SpO2 is 92%, stable for now.
EKG still sinus rhythm. Monitoring 4-lead and 12-lead as needed.
12-lead EKG shows occasional PVCs and signs of possible subendocardial injury.

dialogue.micro_intent: iv start - extremity vein (arm or leg)
dialogue.utterance: IV started in right antecubital vein, 18g. Good access.
Pulse has decreased to 73.
SpO2 is 91% currently.
Blood glucose is 167, so no hypoglycemia.
Temperature is stable at 97.3°F.
EKG shows sinus rhythm with a few PVCs. Otherwise clean.
Respiratory rate went up to 21/min. Temporarily responsive to verbal stimuli.
Administering 1mg Narcan intravenously in the right arm IV, per standing order.
Respirations at 23/min now. Somewhat improved airflow.
Blood pressure is 197/123. Still elevated, but trending up.
SpO2 is 86% - some decline. Need to watch closely.
Blood pressure increased to 200/131, now.
No pain reported. Pain score is 0.

dialogue.micro_intent: iv start - extremity vein (arm or leg)
dialogue.utterance: IV location changed due to swelling - new site on right forearm, 18g.
Administering another 1mg IV Narcan. Suspecting opioid or hypnotic overdose, per standing order.
Respiratory rate is 19/min.
Blood pressure remains at 197/123. Patient shows signs of hypoxia but not hypotension.
ED, incoming patient: 62F, unresponsive with GCS 3 and pinpoint pupils. Suspected opioid or hypnotic abuse.
Received 2mg Narcan initially, IV established in forearm, SpO2 was 86% last. Still labored breathing.
Blood pressure is persistently elevated, and EKG is stable with occasional PVCs.
All interventions per Opioid Overdose Protocol (7-1). Patient secured and ready for transport.
Patient loaded and secured for ED transfer. We've taken him to VCU Health Systems Cold.
Patient was transferred off stretcher using a sheet lift per hospital protocol. Placed on hospital cot in ED.
Soft restraints were applied due to brief agitation before resuming unresponsiveness.
